description: Address code review findings: move frame processing to worker_threads, manage FFmpeg process lifecycle, fix gamut confidence-mask divergence, optimize buffer growth, and fix progress listener leak.
steps: Create electron-app/analysis/analysis-worker.js that uses worker_threads to run ffmpeg spawn and frame processing, posting progress and results back to the main thread
Refactor electron-app/analysis/hdr-analyzer.js analyzeVideo to delegate to the worker, fix gamut confidence-mask threshold (sum > 0.01), and use pre-allocated buffer with write offset
Update electron-app/main.js to store active analysis worker/process reference, kill on window close, and guard against starting a second analysis while one is running
Fix electron-app/preload.js onAnalysisProgress to remove prior listeners before registering new ones
acceptance_criteria: Frame processing runs in a worker_threads Worker, not on the main thread
FFmpeg process is killed when window closes or new analysis starts
Pixels with XYZ sum <= 0.01 are classified as Rec.709
Buffer uses pre-allocated approach instead of Buffer.concat on every chunk
onAnalysisProgress removes existing listeners before adding new one
All modified JS files pass node --check syntax validation
findings: All 5 review issues addressed. Worker thread approach keeps the color science constants and processFrame function duplicated in analysis-worker.js (self-contained worker with no main-thread imports). The MIN_SUM_THRESHOLD constant (0.01) is exported from hdr-analyzer.js for testability. All files pass node --check syntax validation. INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network prevents runtime testing but structural correctness is verified.
